description_long: This object is a low-growing plant with broad, flat leaves radiating outward from a central point, forming a rosette pattern. The leaves are ovate to slightly lanceolate, with smooth edges and a muted green coloration, giving the plant a natural, slightly matte appearance. At the center, several upright, narrower structures suggest the presence of young leaves or undeveloped flower stalks, but no prominent colorful flower head is visible. The plant appears to be herbaceous, with a soft, flexible structure typical of ground-covering vegetation found in urban parks, roadside verges, or landscaped gardens. The surface of the leaves is slightly textured, with visible veins and a matte finish that diffuses light. The overall form is compact and close to the ground, making it suitable for decorative or ecological use in urban green spaces.
description: A compact, ground-hugging plant with broad, matte green leaves radiating from a central point and subtle upright central shoots. The leaves are ovate and slightly textured, forming a dense rosette.
description_view_0: From this angle, the broad, overlapping leaves are clearly visible, radiating outward in a rosette with the central upright shoots partially obscured.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the symmetry of the leaf arrangement, showing the even spread and the central cluster of upright shoots more clearly.
description_view_2: The plant's layered leaf structure is evident here, with the central shoots and the overlapping nature of the leaves visible from a slight side perspective.
description_view_3: This angle highlights the depth of the rosette, with the central upright shoots most prominent and the leaves forming a dense, circular base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals (or leaves, as no distinct flower petals are visible) are arranged in a radial, rosette pattern, overlapping at the base and spreading outward symmetrically from the center.
